feature_concept: useFetch hook for declarative data fetching
scenario: Dashboard page needs to fetch user profile, recent activity, and notifications

v1_junior.what_it_does_well: Simple API, fetches data, returns it
v1_junior.what_breaks: No loading state, no error handling, no cleanup (race conditions when url changes), any type leaks everywhere, no refetch
v1_junior.the_realization: My UI flickers wrong data when navigating fast between pages

v2_mid_level.improvements_over_v1: Loading/error states, generic type, status code check
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Race condition still present, no abort on unmount, no refetch, no cache, no dedupe
v2_mid_level.the_realization: Memory leak warnings in console, stale data overwrites fresh data

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: AbortController prevents race conditions, single state object avoids tearing, refetch function
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No caching across components, no request deduplication, no retry, no suspense support
v3_senior.the_realization: Every component refetches same data, network tab is a disaster

v4_staff.improvements_over_v3: Global cache dedupes requests, useSyncExternalStore for concurrent-safe reads, components share state
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Treats fetching as a shared resource problem, not a component problem. Designed for concurrent React.
v4_staff.the_realization: This is approaching SWR/React Query territory - shared cache changes everything

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Separation of concerns: cache engine vs React adapter. Options-driven behavior (staleTime, retry, select).
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Teaches that the hook is a thin React adapter over a framework-agnostic engine. Same engine can power Vue, Solid, or vanilla JS.
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: When building hooks, ask: what part is React-specific vs universal? Separate them. Test the engine in isolation, the hook becomes trivial.

growth_dimensions: Error handling: silent fail -> typed errors -> AbortError handling -> retry policy -> error boundaries integration
Type safety: any -> generics -> branded keys -> select transform types
Performance: refetch on every mount -> dedupe -> stale-while-revalidate -> background refresh
API design: returns data -> returns state -> returns actions -> options-driven -> framework-agnostic engine
Testability: untestable -> mockable fetch -> swappable engine -> engine testable without React
where_most_engineers_stop: V3 - they add AbortController and feel done. They never realize the cache layer is the real win.
when_to_use_which_version: V2 for prototypes/internal tools. V3 for small apps. V4 when you have 3+ components needing same data. V5 when building a library or your data layer is mission-critical.

scenario: Search bar in a product catalog needs to wait until user stops typing

v1_junior.what_it_does_well: Returns a delayed value, basic API
v1_junior.what_breaks: Timeout never cleared - every keystroke schedules a new update, old timeouts still fire. delay not in deps. Only works for strings.
v1_junior.the_realization: After typing 'macbook' I get 7 setState calls firing over 2 seconds

v2_mid_level.improvements_over_v1: Cleanup clears stale timeouts, generic type, correct deps
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No leading edge option, no max wait (slow typists never get a result), no cancel/flush, no equality check for objects
v2_mid_level.the_realization: User typing slowly never triggers search until they pause - bad UX for paginating

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: Leading edge support, maxWait guarantees responsiveness, refs avoid stale closure
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: Cannot manually flush/cancel, no isPending indicator, hard to test deterministically because of Date.now
v3_senior.the_realization: When form submits I want to flush immediately but I cant - I have to wait the full delay

v4_staff.improvements_over_v3: Returns tuple with controls (flush/cancel/isPending), custom equality, no Date.now needed
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Anticipates real use cases: pending UI, form submit flush, custom equality for objects. Controls are first-class.
v4_staff.the_realization: This is now genuinely reusable across the codebase - search, autosave, validation all use it

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Built on top of useDebouncedCallback primitive. Single source of truth for timing logic. Composable.
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Identifies the underlying primitive (debounced callback) and builds higher-level hooks from it. One bug fix, all hooks improve.
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Find the primitive. Hooks that feel similar usually share an underlying mechanism - extract it. Composition over duplication.

growth_dimensions: Error handling: N/A here, but timer cleanup goes from absent -> on unmount -> on dep change -> on flush/cancel
Type safety: string-only -> generic T -> custom equality typed against T
Performance: leaked timers -> single timer -> max wait guard -> built on shared primitive
API design: returns value -> returns value -> returns value+pending -> returns value+controls -> composed from primitive
Testability: timing-dependent and flaky -> uses fake timers -> exposes flush for deterministic tests
where_most_engineers_stop: V2 - they add cleanup and ship. They never hit the slow-typist or flush-on-submit problems until production.
when_to_use_which_version: V2 for one-off search inputs. V3 when you need maxWait. V4 when multiple features need flush/cancel. V5 when you have a family of timing hooks.

scenario: User preferences (theme, sidebar collapsed, language) persisted across sessions

v1_junior.what_it_does_well: Reads from storage, writes on change
v1_junior.what_breaks: Crashes on SSR (no window), no JSON support, no sync across tabs, no error handling for quota, string-only
v1_junior.the_realization: Next.js build fails with ReferenceError: localStorage is not defined

v2_mid_level.improvements_over_v1: SSR-safe, JSON support, generic type, try/catch
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Hydration mismatch (initial render uses default, useEffect updates after), no tab sync, swallows errors silently
v2_mid_level.the_realization: Theme flashes light then dark on every page load - terrible UX

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: Lazy init avoids hydration flash on CSR, cross-tab sync, functional updater, error logged
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: Still hydration mismatch on SSR, no schema validation (corrupt data crashes app), key changes leak listeners, no serializer customization
v3_senior.the_realization: User upgraded the app, old localStorage shape crashes parse - whole app whitescreens

v4_staff.improvements_over_v3: Pluggable serializer/validator (Zod-friendly), survives corrupt data, swappable storage for testing
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Treats validation as essential. Storage is injectable for tests. Forward-compatible with schema evolution.
v4_staff.the_realization: Adding Zod validation prevents the entire class of corrupt-data crashes

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Adapter pattern abstracts storage backend. useSyncExternalStore gives SSR-correct hydration. Renamed to reflect intent.
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Teaches that 'localStorage' is an implementation detail. The concept is persistent state. The right abstraction is an adapter interface.
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: When naming a hook, name the CAPABILITY not the API it wraps. useLocalStorage ties you to localStorage. usePersistentState lets you swap backends. Names are architecture.

growth_dimensions: Error handling: crash on SSR -> swallowed errors -> logged errors -> validation fallback -> typed codec failures
Type safety: string -> generic -> Zod schema -> codec interface
Performance: read on every render -> lazy init -> useSyncExternalStore
API design: useState-like -> + functional updater -> + options -> + adapter pattern
Testability: ties to localStorage -> swappable storage -> mockable adapter for unit tests
where_most_engineers_stop: V3 - lazy init + storage event feels complete. They never imagine swapping backends until they need to.
when_to_use_which_version: V2 for client-only SPAs. V3 for most production apps. V4 when you have schema versioning concerns. V5 when building cross-platform (RN + web).

feature_concept: Login form with email and password
scenario: User authentication form on a SaaS marketing site

v1_junior.what_breaks: No form element (no Enter to submit), password not type=password, no loading state, no error display, no validation, button submits twice on double click, no CSRF
v1_junior.the_realization: Users complain Enter doesnt work, and theyre seeing their passwords in plain text

v2_mid_level.improvements_over_v1: Form element, types, loading state, error display, basic HTML validation
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No accessible labels (only placeholders), no field-level errors, no aria-invalid, error message not announced, hardcoded redirect, no field focus on error
v2_mid_level.the_realization: Screen reader users cant tell what field is what, and a11y audit fails

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: Zod validation, accessible labels, aria-invalid, role=alert, focus on error, dependency injection via prop
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: Validation logic mixed with UI, form state is ad-hoc, every form will reimplement this, no controlled vs uncontrolled story
v3_senior.the_realization: Building the third form, I realize Im copy-pasting validation patterns - error-prone

v4_staff.improvements_over_v3: Extracted useForm hook + Field component, schema is source of truth, autoComplete hints, form is now declarative
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: The component reads like a spec. All form mechanics live in useForm/Field where they can be tested once and reused everywhere.
v4_staff.the_realization: Building forms is now boring - which is exactly what we want

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Form/Field/SubmitButton are platform primitives. Schemas live in /schemas. Auth lives in /auth. Component is purely declarative.
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Separation of concerns by layer: domain (schema), platform (Form), feature (LoginForm). Each layer changes for different reasons.
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: A feature component should be SHORT. If its long, the platform is missing something. Long feature components are a smell that the platform isnt pulling its weight.

growth_dimensions: Error handling: success-only -> caught network -> field-level + submit -> typed schema errors -> auth-domain errors
Type safety: any strings -> typed state -> Zod schema -> z.infer flowing through -> branded auth types
Performance: rerender on every keystroke -> stable handlers -> uncontrolled with refs in useForm
API design: inline handlers -> form element -> hook + Field -> platform primitives
Testability: untestable network call -> mockable fetch -> useForm tested independently -> auth layer tested independently
where_most_engineers_stop: V3 - Zod + a11y feels professional. They never extract useForm because each form feels different until you build three.
when_to_use_which_version: V2 for marketing landing pages. V3 for one-off forms. V4 when you have 3+ forms. V5 when forms are core to your product (SaaS, internal tools).

feature_concept: Multi-step signup wizard with validation per step
scenario: SaaS onboarding: account info -> company details -> plan selection

v1_junior.what_breaks: No validation, no back button, refresh loses data, no progress indicator, any type, no error handling, double submit possible
v1_junior.the_realization: User accidentally refreshes mid-signup and loses all input

v2_mid_level.improvements_over_v1: Types, back button, progress text, basic validation
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Refresh still loses data, validation is inline ad-hoc, no schema per step, no a11y, hard to add/remove steps
v2_mid_level.the_realization: Product wants to A/B test step order - my switch statement is a nightmare

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: Per-step schemas, sessionStorage persistence, a11y, derived steps array
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: Each step inline, hard to add per-step layouts, no global state for shared validation, no analytics events, no async step (e.g., check email available)
v3_senior.the_realization: Step 2 needs an async check against the API, my sync schema cant handle it cleanly

v4_staff.improvements_over_v3: Step is a config (schema + component + async validator), wizard is reusable, context shares state
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Wizard is data-driven. Adding/reordering/A-B testing steps is trivial. Async validation per step is first-class.
v4_staff.the_realization: PM asks to add 'How did you hear about us?' step - takes 5 min, not 2 hours

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: State machine makes flow explicit and testable. URL is source of truth. Step registry is purely declarative.
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Models the wizard as a state machine - the right primitive for multi-step flows. Invalid states cannot exist by construction.
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: When you find yourself with many booleans (isValidating, isSubmitting, hasError, isComplete), youre simulating a state machine badly. Use the real thing. State machines turn untestable UI flows into testable graphs.

growth_dimensions: Error handling: none -> inline -> per-step schema -> async validation per step -> state-machine guards
Type safety: any -> step types -> z.infer per step -> wizard generic -> machine context typed
Performance: keep all steps mounted -> render one -> lazy steps -> code-split per step
API design: imperative steps -> step config -> step components -> state machine + registry
Testability: untestable -> mock fetch -> test useWizard -> test machine without UI
where_most_engineers_stop: V3 - sessionStorage + Zod feels mature. They reinvent state machine logic with booleans until it collapses.
when_to_use_which_version: V2 for 2-3 step prototypes. V3 for stable wizards. V4 when steps may be reordered. V5 when the flow has branching, conditional steps, or compliance requirements.

feature_concept: Rendering a list of items with selection

v1_junior.what_breaks: Can select same id twice, no deselect, O(n) on every includes, no keyboard nav, no a11y, inline styles, list rerenders entirely on selection
v1_junior.the_realization: With 200 emails, clicking feels laggy and selecting same email 3 times shows '3 selected'

v2_mid_level.improvements_over_v1: Set for O(1) lookup, toggle works, Tailwind, useCallback
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Whole list rerenders on each toggle, no keyboard, no a11y role, no shift-click range, item component not memoized
v2_mid_level.the_realization: Selecting an item rerenders all 1000 emails - profile shows wasted ms

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: Memoized item, keyboard support, ARIA roles, focus ring
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: 10k emails still slow (DOM size), no virtualization, no shift-click range select, selection state can drift if emails change, no roving tabindex
v3_senior.the_realization: Customer with 50k emails, scrolling stutters - DOM cant handle it

v4_staff.improvements_over_v3: Virtualized (handles 1M rows), shift-click range, roving tabindex
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Right primitive for the scale (virtualization). Selection logic supports real keyboard patterns. Composable hooks.
v4_staff.the_realization: Scrolls 50k smooth, keyboard nav matches Gmail

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Decoupled selection model from list. Both are framework-level primitives. Feature code is purely declarative.
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Identifies two orthogonal concerns (rendering vs selection) and gives each a primitive. Combinatorial reuse.
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: When two concerns travel together in components, ask: are they really one concern, or two that always co-occur? If two, split them - youll unlock reuse you didnt see coming.

growth_dimensions: Error handling: N/A; but selection sync when items change goes from broken -> ok -> via key-based model
Type safety: untyped -> typed Email -> generic item -> generic selection model
Performance: O(n) selection lookups + full rerender -> Set + memo -> virtualization
API design: inline -> internal -> hook -> primitive -> two composable primitives
Testability: tied to DOM size -> memoized units testable -> selection model testable in isolation
where_most_engineers_stop: V3 - memo + a11y feels enough. They never virtualize until a customer complains.
when_to_use_which_version: V2 lists <50 items. V3 lists <500. V4 lists >500 or need shift-click. V5 when you have many list-like UIs across the app.

scenario: Confirm delete dialog, settings panel, image preview

v1_junior.what_breaks: No Escape, no focus trap, scrolls behind, no portal (z-index war), background tabbable, no a11y role, no animation
v1_junior.the_realization: Tab key leaves modal, screen readers ignore the overlay - a11y is broken

v2_mid_level.improvements_over_v1: Portal, Escape, body scroll lock, role=dialog
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No focus trap (Tab escapes), no return-focus on close, no aria-labelledby, multiple modals fight each other, no animation
v2_mid_level.the_realization: Tab still escapes the modal, and after closing focus is on body - keyboard users lost

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: Focus trap, return focus on close, labelledBy support
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: Focus trap is naive (misses dynamic content, iframes, contenteditable), no animation, no stacking, no inert background, doesnt handle SSR
v3_senior.the_realization: Adding a dynamically-loaded form inside the modal breaks focus trap because focusable list is captured once

v4_staff.improvements_over_v3: Robust focus trap (focus-trap-react), inert background, animation, sizes, SSR-safe
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Uses inert for true background isolation. Composable, animated, SSR-safe. Production-grade.
v4_staff.the_realization: This passes axe-core and works for real users with assistive tech

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Compound component over Radix - all a11y patterns are battle-tested. Style via className, behavior is locked correct.
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Acknowledges that focus trap + a11y + SSR + RTL + portal + animation is a full-time job. Delegates to a primitive lib and styles on top.
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: For widgets with hard a11y (dialog, popover, menu, combobox, tabs), use a headless primitive library. Your code becomes a style sheet over correctness. Rebuilding these is a years-long mistake.

growth_dimensions: Error handling: no escape -> escape -> focus loops -> focus trap -> background isolation
Type safety: untyped children -> Props interface -> compound generics from Radix
Performance: always mounted -> conditional -> portal -> animated mount/unmount
API design: open prop -> compound -> compound on primitive library
Testability: clicks on overlay only -> mockable callbacks -> testing via Radix testing patterns -> a11y guaranteed by lib
where_most_engineers_stop: V3 - they wire up their own focus trap and ship. It fails in production for dynamic content and they patch it forever.
when_to_use_which_version: V2 for non-public internal modals. V3 if you must avoid deps. V4 when you have time but no Radix. V5 always when possible - this is the right answer for real products.

scenario: Admin dashboard listing customers with sort by name/created/revenue

v1_junior.what_breaks: Mutates a copy fine but sort is unstable, any type, no direction toggle, no pagination, no a11y, dates compared as strings
v1_junior.the_realization: With 5000 rows the page hangs for 2 seconds on every sort click

v2_mid_level.improvements_over_v1: Types, memoized sort, dir toggle, pagination
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Client-side only (huge datasets), no filtering, dates still string-compared, columns hardcoded, no a11y for sort, no resize/reorder
v2_mid_level.the_realization: Loading 100k rows from API breaks - we need server-driven

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: Generic, column config, server-driven via React Query, aria-sort, placeholderData for smooth paging
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No filtering, no column reorder/resize, no row selection, no virtual scrolling, URL state not synced (back button broken)
v3_senior.the_realization: User refreshes page after sorting - sort is gone. Back button doesnt restore state.

v4_staff.improvements_over_v3: TanStack Table for column model, URL state via nuqs (back button + share links work), filter integrated
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Composes industry-standard primitives. URL is source of truth = shareable, refreshable, history-correct.
v4_staff.the_realization: PMs share links to filtered/sorted views - whole team collaborates faster

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Domain hook (useDataTable) returns a model. UI is purely presentational. Same model can power non-table views.
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Inverts the relationship: data state isnt 'inside' the table component, the table is a view on a data model. The model can render as table, cards, grid, export.
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: When a component owns lots of state, ask: would it make sense to extract a non-UI hook that returns that state? If yes, you may have a domain concept hiding inside a UI component.

growth_dimensions: Error handling: silent on bad sort -> typed sort keys -> graceful query errors -> retry + suspense
Type safety: any -> typed rows -> generic columns -> ColumnDef<T> with accessors
Performance: client sort 100k -> server sort -> placeholderData -> virtualized rows
API design: inline -> generic prop API -> URL-driven -> hook + slots
Testability: tied to data fixture -> mockable fetcher -> testable column defs -> testable model independent of UI
where_most_engineers_stop: V3 - generic table feels like a final form. They never wire URL state until the back button bug bites.
when_to_use_which_version: V2 for fixed admin pages. V3 for one table per page. V4 when shareable links matter. V5 when tables are a core surface (analytics, admin, ops).

feature_concept: Search input that filters/queries a list as user types

v1_junior.what_breaks: Case sensitive, no debounce for server, no async

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: For server queries: no abort, no loading, no error

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No history, no a11y combobox

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Full keyboard + history + a11y
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Combobox WAI-ARIA + UX features composable
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Search is a complete pattern, not just an input

growth_dimensions: Error handling: none → cancel → query error → graceful
Type safety: implicit → generic → fully typed
Performance: filter render → debounce → cancel → placeholder
API design: monolithic → hook → composable headless
Testability: hard → moderate → easy via hook → independent test
where_most_engineers_stop: V2-V3. Server async + debounce considered 'done'. Missing keyboard + a11y often.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: prototypes. V2-V3: typical apps. V4: design system with multiple search surfaces. V5: when search is a core product feature with power-user expectations.

v1_junior.code: <input type='file' onChange={e => { const f = e.target.files[0]; const fd = new FormData(); fd.append('file', f); fetch('/api/upload', { method: 'POST', body: fd }); }} />
v1_junior.what_breaks: No validation, no progress, no error

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No retry, no chunking for huge files, no resume

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No resume, no chunking

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Standard protocol (tus); survives reloads via local resume
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Server-agnostic protocol; resumes across sessions
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Uploads as protocol problem; standards over rolling own

growth_dimensions: Error: none → catch → retry → resumable
Type safety: any → typed → generic
Performance: blocking → progress → chunked → resumable
API: inline → component → class → protocol
Testability: hard → moderate → service-level → integration
where_most_engineers_stop: V2-V3. Most uploads stop at progress + retry. Resumability ignored.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: prototypes. V2: simple files <10MB. V3: typical uploads. V4: large files. V5: video/very-large/critical reliability.

v1_junior.what_breaks: No keys, no a11y, no nav, no preload

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No focus trap, no portal, no swipe, no preload, hydration

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No swipe, no preload of next image

v4_staff.the_realization: Image zoom for product use case

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Zoom, captions, deep-linking, fullscreen, generic
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Lightbox is reusable primitive across product photos, blog images, etc.
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Reusable primitives > one-off implementations

growth_dimensions: Error: none → image load fallback → preload optimization
Type: implicit → typed → generic
Performance: load all → lazy → preload neighbors → zoom-aware
API: monolithic → composed → generic primitive
Testability: hard → easier with library → primitive testable
where_most_engineers_stop: V2-V3. Most ship 'works' lightbox without swipe/preload/deep-linking.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: prototype. V2-V3: typical. V4: mobile-heavy product. V5: product is image-centric (e-commerce, blog with rich media).

v1_junior.what_breaks: No keyboard, no animation, jank

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: react-beautiful-dnd unmaintained; SSR issues

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: Mobile feel, optimistic save

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Cross-container, sensor configuration, accessibility announcements, drag overlay
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Full kanban experience matches Trello/Jira-level UX
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Accessible DnD is feasible; library + thoughtful config = production-grade

growth_dimensions: Error: ignore → rollback → telemetry
Type: any → typed → generic constraints
Performance: HTML5 → library → virtualized
API: inline → component → primitive
Testability: hard → library helpers → mockable sensors
where_most_engineers_stop: V2-V3. Library + sortable feels enough. Touch + a11y skipped.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never ship. V2: legacy (avoid react-beautiful-dnd). V3: typical app. V4: mobile-first. V5: complex flows like kanban.

v1_junior.what_breaks: No multiple, no auto-dismiss, no positioning

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No imperative API, no SR announce, in App scope

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No animation, no priority/queueing, no swipe-dismiss

v4_staff.the_realization: Toast taxonomy: success/error/info/loading with promise integration

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Promise integration, mutation auto-toast
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Toasts become an integration with async state lib, not just UI
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Notifications integrate with data lifecycle

growth_dimensions: Error: silent → ID-based dismiss → animated → integrated
Type: any → typed → generic mutation
Performance: setTimeout → AnimatePresence
API: useState → Context → imperative
Testability: hard → providers → library mocks
where_most_engineers_stop: V3. Provider+hook+a11y considered done. Animation, priorities skipped.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2: prototypes. V3: typical apps. V4: design system. V5: data-heavy apps with many mutations.

v1_junior.what_breaks: Token in localStorage (XSS), no expiry handling, no refresh

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No session refresh, no logout, no protected routes

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: Session via React Query + protected routes
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No refresh on 401, no multi-tab sync

v4_staff.the_realization: Edge cases: idle timeout, concurrent refreshes, SSO

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: State machine for clarity, concurrent refresh dedup, idle timeout
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Auth is a state machine; explicit transitions; visualizable
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Auth is one of the canonical state machine domains

growth_dimensions: Error: silent → user-shown → 401-recovery → SM-driven
Security: localStorage → httpOnly → CSRF → idle
Performance: per-request login check → cached session → cross-tab
API: imperative → context → state machine
Testability: hard → mockable fetch → SM-testable
where_most_engineers_stop: V3. Session + protected routes feels done. Refresh + tab sync + idle skipped.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2-V3: typical apps. V4: production-grade. V5: high-security (banking, healthcare).

v1_junior.what_breaks: No persistence, no system pref, no CSS-driven

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No system pref, SSR flash, no smooth transition

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: System pref + responds to OS change + no FOUC
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: Hard-coded 2 themes; no multi-brand

v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Tokens drive everything; runtime brand swap

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Auto-derived dark variants from light; multi-brand without per-mode JSON
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Theming is values + derivation; one set of brand colors expands to full palette
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Theming = math on colors, not duplicated palette files

growth_dimensions: Error: none → SSR-safe → matchMedia listener
Type: any → 'light'|'dark' → branded
Performance: re-render → CSS vars → derivation cached
API: state → hook → multi-tenant
Testability: hard → mockable matchMedia → snapshot tokens
where_most_engineers_stop: V3. Toggle + persistence + system feels done. Tokens unused.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2: simple apps. V3: typical product. V4: design system with multiple brands or tenants. V5: white-label SaaS.

v1_junior.what_breaks: No URL, no back button, no deep linking

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No guards, no preload, no SSR

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No code splitting, no preload, all routes load

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Loaders parallel-load, deferred for streaming, lazy routes auto-split
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Routing + data + code splitting integrated
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Router is the layer for data loading, not just URL matching

growth_dimensions: Error: crash → 404 → errorElement → granular
Type: implicit → typed → route param types
Performance: all bundled → split → preload → streaming
API: state → router → integrated
Testability: hard → MemoryRouter → loader mocking
where_most_engineers_stop: V3-V4. Guards + lazy considered done. Loaders + streaming skipped.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2: simple. V3: typical with auth. V4: perf-conscious. V5: data-heavy app.

v1_junior.what_breaks: User unaware, no telemetry

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No retry, no classify, render errors uncaught

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: Single boundary; granular needed

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Pluggable handler pipeline, per-error-type strategy
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Errors are a pipeline: classify, attempt recovery, fall through to user
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Errors are first-class architectural concern

growth_dimensions: Visibility: silent → toast → telemetry → pipeline
Recovery: none → retry → circuit → policy
Type: untyped → Error → custom classes → pipeline contracts
API: try/catch → boundary → middleware
Testability: hard → boundaries mockable → pipeline composable
where_most_engineers_stop: V2-V3. Toast + ErrorBoundary considered done. Recovery skipped.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2: prototypes. V3: typical apps. V4: high-traffic. V5: critical systems (fintech, healthcare).

v1_junior.what_breaks: Dev only

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No component-level, no segmentation

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No correlations, manual analysis

v4_staff.the_realization: Custom business metrics + perf budget enforcement

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Distributed tracing across business flows; CI perf budgets
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Performance is a contract enforced in CI; traces follow business flows
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Observability is built in from the start, not bolted on

growth_dimensions: Visibility: console → vitals → React profiler → RUM → traces
Sampling: full → percentage → adaptive
API: ad-hoc → telemetry layer → traced flows
Testability: hard → mockable telemetry → snapshot traces
Enforcement: ignored → dashboards → CI gates
where_most_engineers_stop: V2-V3. Web Vitals reported. No traces or budgets.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: dev only. V2: every product. V3: perf-sensitive. V4: production-critical. V5: business-flow critical.

v1_junior.what_breaks: SSR crash, strings only, no cross-tab

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No cross-tab sync, no quota handling

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No serialization customization, no validation

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Versioning + migrations for schema evolution
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Storage as versioned schema; survives app evolution
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Persistent state needs migration strategy, like a database

growth_dimensions: Error: crash → fallback → telemetry → versioned cleanup
Type: any → typed → schema-validated → migrated
Performance: re-read → cached → throttled
API: 2-arg → options → composable
Testability: hard → mockable storage → snapshot migrations
where_most_engineers_stop: V2-V3. JSON + SSR-safe considered done. Versioning ignored.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: prototypes. V2-V3: typical. V4: production with mixed clients. V5: long-lived apps with evolving schema.

v1_junior.what_breaks: No sort, no filter, no pages, no keys

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No pagination, no virtualization, no column config

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No virtualization, all data in memory

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Virtualized infinite scroll with full a11y
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Table scales to 100K+ rows with proper a11y
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Table is composition of: data fetching + virtualization + selection + a11y - each independent

growth_dimensions: Error: silent → error UI → loading skeleton
Type: any → typed → generic ColumnDef
Performance: render all → memo → virtualized
API: monolithic → component → headless + composable
Testability: hard → headless API testable
where_most_engineers_stop: V3-V4. TanStack Table considered enough. Virtualization + server-driven skipped.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2: small lists. V3: <500 rows. V4: typical admin tables. V5: large datasets.

v1_junior.what_breaks: No loading, no error, no cancel

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Race, leak on unmount, no cache

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No cache, no refetch, no stale-while-revalidate

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Schema validation, observability, tag-based invalidation
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Hook factory pattern for type-safe domain APIs
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Data fetching is a domain layer, not a util

growth_dimensions: Error: silent → caught → cancelled → typed → observable
Type: any → typed → generic → schema-validated
Performance: re-fetch always → cached → stale-while-revalidate
API: 1 hook → typed → factory
Testability: hard → mockable → MSW-tested
where_most_engineers_stop: V3-V4. React Query is the typical end. Schema validation skipped.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2: trivial. V3: rare; just use V4. V4: typical. V5: large API surface area.

v1_junior.what_breaks: No validation, no error, no loading

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Validation duplicated; no a11y; no schema

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No autocomplete, password strength, oauth

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Composable form primitive with draft persistence + schema-driven
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Form = schema + composable Field components + draft layer
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Forms are domain objects with schema, drafts, validation as separable concerns

growth_dimensions: Error: server-side → field-level → composable
Type: untyped → schema-inferred
Performance: re-render all → RHF refs → memoized
API: ad-hoc → schema-driven → composable
Testability: hard → schema-mocked → RHF-tested
where_most_engineers_stop: V3. RHF + Zod considered done.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2: simple. V3: typical. V4: conversion-critical. V5: many forms in design system.

v1_junior.what_breaks: No a11y, no portal, no focus, no escape

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No focus trap, no body scroll lock, no aria-labelledby

v3_senior.improvements_over_v2: Radix - focus trap, body lock, aria-labelledby, escape
v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No animation, no responsive, no stacked modals

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Imperative API for sequential confirm flows
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Modals as a promise-returning service, not just declarative components
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Imperative APIs valid for sequential async UX patterns

growth_dimensions: A11y: none → role/escape → Radix complete
Performance: render hidden → conditional
API: declarative → imperative service
Animation: none → AnimatePresence → responsive
Testability: hard → Radix-tested → service mockable
where_most_engineers_stop: V3. Radix-based modals considered done.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2: prototypes. V3: typical. V4: polished consumer. V5: many confirmation flows.

v1_junior.code:
{messages.map(m =>
  • {m.text}
    • )}
v1_junior.what_breaks: Slow at 1000+ items

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Still renders all - slow at 10K

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: Variable heights, no scroll-to-bottom, no infinite

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Scroll preservation on prepend, role=log for a11y
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Chat list = virtualization + scroll preservation + load older + a11y; each composable
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: List virtualization is the foundation; chat is an application of it

growth_dimensions: Performance: linear → memo → virtual → variable-height
A11y: list → log + aria-live
API: monolithic → composable
Type: any → generic
Testability: hard → virtualizer-mockable
where_most_engineers_stop: V3. Virtualized considered done. Chat-specifics skipped.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: small lists. V2: <500 items. V3: 500-10K static. V4: dynamic feed. V5: chat-like.

v1_junior.what_breaks: Spam, no debounce, no state

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No status, no error, no optimistic

v3_senior.what_still_isnt_great: No status indicator UX

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Per-field state + etag conflict detection
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Auto-save handles concurrent edits via etags; granular status
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Auto-save + multiplayer needs conflict detection (etags or CRDTs)

growth_dimensions: Save: fire-forget → debounced → optimistic → conflict-aware
Status: none → status state → per-field
Type: implicit → typed → generic
API: imperative → mutation → hook
Testability: hard → hook-tested
where_most_engineers_stop: V3. Optimistic + debounce.
when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2-V3: typical settings. V4: polished. V5: multi-user collaborative settings.
